task: I’m getting ready for a period when I’ll be handling everything on my own, and I want to make sure the basics don’t slip through the cracks. Can you help me think through a simple way to keep track of regular responsibilities as they come up?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. A simple system is best here—something you can glance at quickly and update without much effort.

Here’s an easy way to track regular responsibilities:

### 1) Make 3 buckets
Keep everything in one place, but sort it into:

- **Do first** — anything with a real deadline, appointment, bill due date, or time-sensitive issue
- **Do soon** — important but not urgent
- **Keep an eye on** — things that may need attention later

### 2) Use one running list
A notebook, phone note, or paper calendar is fine. For each item, write:

- **What it is**
- **Due date / when it needs attention**
- **Next step**
- **Status**: not started / waiting / done

Example:
- Electric bill — due 5/10 — pay online — not started
- Doctor appointment — 5/8 at 2 PM — confirm ride — waiting
- Chargeback notice — check for actual deadline — save document first — not started

### 3) Check it at the same time each day
A short daily check is enough:
- What is due **today or tomorrow**
- What needs a call, email, or payment
- What can wait

### 4) Keep “proof” with the task
For anything important, save:
- the notice or letter
- screenshots
- confirmation numbers
- names, dates, and times of calls

That way you’re not relying on memory or a phone promise.

### 5) Don’t let small tasks pile up
If something takes under 5 minutes, do it right away if you can:
- reply to message
- set reminder
- file paper
- add appointment to calendar

### 6) Use a weekly reset
Once a week, spend 10–15 minutes:
- review bills
- check appointments
- look for anything with a deadline
- move items into the right bucket

If you want, I can help you turn this into a very simple template you can copy into your phone or notebook.

user_simulator.text: That’s fine, but it’s still a little broad. I’d want an actual plain template I can use without fuss — something like a one-page list with maybe a date, what it is, what’s due, and whether it’s done. And I’d want it set up for bills, appointments, and any letters or notices separately. Keep it simple. No fancy app nonsense.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a plain one-page template you can copy into a notebook or single sheet of paper.

## Simple Responsibility Tracker

### Bills
| Date | What it is | Due / action needed | Done? |
|------|------------|---------------------|-------|
|      |            |                     | [ ]   |
|      |            |                     | [ ]   |
|      |            |                     | [ ]   |

### Appointments
| Date | What it is | Time / location | Done? |
|------|------------|-----------------|-------|
|      |            |                 | [ ]   |
|      |            |                 | [ ]   |
|      |            |                 | [ ]   |

### Letters / Notices
| Date received | What it is | What it says to do | Done? |
|---------------|------------|--------------------|-------|
|               |            |                    | [ ]   |
|               |            |                    | [ ]   |
|               |            |                    | [ ]   |

### Quick check
At the top or bottom of the page, you can add:

- **Today’s date:** __________
- **Needs attention first:** ____________________
- **Waiting on someone else:** ____________________

If you want, I can also turn this into an even tighter version with just 3 columns per section.

user_simulator.text: That works. Plain enough. I can use that without fuss.
